Title: Starch phosphorylation regulates starch granule morphological homogeneity in Arabidopsis thaliana

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of DPE2, together with SEX4 deficiency, disrupts starch granule morphological homogeneity and starch turnover in leaves.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DPE2 was a core experimentally manipulated gene used to generate the dpe2sex4 double mutant. 2. Loss of DPE2 together with SEX4 produced two coexisting starch granule populations, small and large, within the same chloroplast under 12 h light/12 h dark conditions. 3. The double mutant showed reduced starch degradation and smaller rosettes, while continuous light restored morphologically homogeneous starch granules.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic knockout/crossing of dpe2-5 with sex4-3; native gel activity assay for DPE2 loss; SEM and LCSM of starch granules; starch and phosphate quantification; growth under light/dark versus continuous light
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytosolic disproportionating enzyme 2; glucanotransferase-like starch metabolism enzyme
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12 h light/12 h dark versus continuous light; transfer from diurnal cycle to continuous light
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DPE2 is not a catalytic enzyme acting directly on the starch granule surface in chloroplasts but its loss is decisive in combination with SEX4 for abnormal starch granule homogeneity. dpe2 single mutant alone had larger, rounder yet homogeneous granules.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Smaller rosette and reduced fresh weight in the double mutant; impaired starch turnover
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering starch granule morphology via degradation-pathway perturbation, but likely accompanied by growth penalties and abnormal carbon turnover.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major transient carbon storage in leaves

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SEX4 deficiency in combination with DPE2 loss alters starch phosphorylation-linked granule morphology and homogeneity.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SEX4 was a core experimentally manipulated gene in the dpe2sex4 double mutant. 2. The combined absence of SEX4 and DPE2 caused small and large starch granules to coexist in the same chloroplast under light/dark cycles. 3. Continuous light removed the heterogeneity, indicating that the SEX4-related effect depends on the dark phase and starch degradation context.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic knockout/crossing of sex4-3 with dpe2-5; western blot for loss of SEX4; SEM/LCSM; starch phosphate and glucan analyses under contrasting light regimes
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glucan phosphatase; dual-specificity phosphatase family with carbohydrate-binding function
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12 h light/12 h dark versus continuous light; delayed transfer to continuous light
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: sex4 single mutant alone accumulated larger and rounder but still homogeneous granules. The paper links altered C6 phosphate utilization and soluble phosphoglucan accumulation to the double-mutant phenotype rather than simple starch excess alone.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Growth reduction in double mutant context; defective starch breakdown and phosphoglucan accumulation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for modulating starch phosphorylation and granule properties, but manipulation may impair degradation and plant growth.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major transient carbon reserve in leaves

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: enzyme from Arabidopsis used in vitro on dpe2sex4 leaf starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Granule-bound/soluble glucan elongation on starch granule surface
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucosyl residues from ADP-glucose to glucan chains in starch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: In vitro, SS1 incorporated fewer glucosyl residues per unit granule surface into small dpe2sex4 granules than into large granules, indicating reduced synthetic competence of small granules.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SS1 was used as a starch-synthesizing enzyme to test whether small and large granules differ in synthetic competence. 2. Although total incorporation signal reflected greater surface area of small granules, normalization to granule surface showed lower SS1-mediated incorporation on small granules. 3. This supports the conclusion that the compact small-granule surface hinders starch synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro [14C]ADP-glucose incorporation assay with recombinant AtSS1 on isolated native starch granule fractions; normalization by calculated surface area
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assay; phenotype interpreted under light/dark versus continuous light growth regimes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports use of granule-surface properties as engineering targets to modulate starch deposition efficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SS1 was not a mutated core causal gene for phenotype generation, but it was directly used to functionally validate that the small granule surface is less permissive for starch synthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: enzyme from Arabidopsis used in vitro on dpe2sex4 leaf starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds glucosyl residues from glucose-1-phosphate to α-glucan chains on starch granules in the synthetic direction used here
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PHS1 incorporated fewer glucosyl residues per unit granule surface into small dpe2sex4 granules than into large granules, supporting reduced accessibility/competence of small granules for starch synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PHS1 was used to probe surface-dependent glucosyl incorporation into isolated granule fractions. 2. Normalized [14C]glucosyl incorporation was lower for small than for large granules. 3. The result independently confirmed that small granules are relatively inert for starch synthesis under the mutant condition.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro [14C]Glc-1-P incorporation assay with AtPHS1 on isolated starch granules; surface-area normalization
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assay; relevant to light/dark versus continuous light physiological states
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a biochemical probe for starch granule surface accessibility; may aid engineering of starch deposition properties.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PHS1 was used for mechanistic validation rather than as the causal mutated gene of the phenotype.
